git_releases.summary: Wan2.2 Vast workflow machine-registry production path

runtime_strategies.description: Use the base Vast Comfy image and do not treat known machines as cache.
Prefer previously successful machines and enable WarmStart only when one is selected.

workflow_branches.notes: Single-segment direct output path. This is the current stable production branch.

Segmented generation path. v3 single-instance remains the default candidate after verified 30s and 60s Vast runs. v4 anchor-overlap produced real 30s output after manual rescue but failed visual frame review because later segments re-generated different composition and identity; do not use v4 for 30s/60s production.

workflow_branches.source_workflow: workflows/书墨-30s长视频-wan2-2AnimateKJ版_v2版-参考动作、表情.json
workflow_branches.notes: 2.0 KJ single-shot 30s branch. It uses a pure-color transparent IP image plus a 30s reference video, redraws the background from prompt, and avoids segmented stitching. Initial 30s validation succeeded, but cold-start and inference cost are high, so this is a candidate branch rather than the production default.

workflow_branches.source_workflow: workflows/书墨-30s长视频-wan2-2AnimateKJ版_v2版-参考动作、表情.json
workflow_branches.notes: Experimental KJ long-duration wrapper. It splits input video into 30s chunks, runs the existing KJ 30s workflow sequentially on one Vast instance, downloads each segment, and merges with local ffmpeg concat. It does not add a ComfyUI merge plugin and does not provide continue_motion continuity because this KJ workflow has no continue_motion input.

implementation_variants.description: Single-shot KJ 30s implementation. Stages a fixed transparent-background IP image and a 30s reference video, converts the KJ canvas workflow to API JSON, disables torch compile on RTX 3090, runs one 481-frame 720x720 prompt, downloads the real VHS output node, and publishes the result. It now runs a HuggingFace speed preflight before bootstrap and includes a 4090 candidate selector that lists 10-20 offers, short-rents 1-3 per batch, and continues to the next batch when none pass.

validated_runs.notes: Generation succeeded after disabling node 137 WanVideoTorchCompileSettings and removing WanVideoModelLoader.compile_args. The output was downloaded and manually published because the first run hit a Windows GBK vastai logs encoding issue during timing summarization; the runner was patched afterward.

implementation_variants.description: Splits videos longer than 30s into 30s chunks, stages per-segment KJ runtime workflows, runs them sequentially on one Vast instance via the existing KJ remote submit script, then downloads and ffmpeg-concats the segment outputs. The branch now supports optional 2.0 B2 background anchoring: keep the pure IP image as person reference, load one prompt-generated bg_image.png, repeat it to frame_load_cap, and connect bg_images plus an IP-alpha-derived mask to WanVideoAnimateEmbeds. The branch also treats reference-video overlays as a probabilistic quality risk and uses preflight reference-overlay scanning plus postflight generated-artifact review before accepting long-video outputs. Cleanup roadmap: 2.0 keeps rule-based detection and targeted local cleaning; 2.1 implements a general reference-video pollution preprocessor and evaluates video-level inpainting such as ProPainter/E2FGVI; 2.2 will evaluate lighter image/local repair such as LaMa/MAT, Crop & Stitch, and SAM/OCR-assisted masks.

b2_background_conditioning.ip_image: 素材资产/美女图无背景纯色/纯色坐着.png
b2_background_conditioning.mask: LoadImageMask alpha from ip_image.png -> InvertMask -> GrowMask

validated_runs.notes: Initial two-segment output technically merged but failed visual review because segment 2 hallucinated a standing duplicate/background person. The live instance was kept and only segment 2 was rerun with a new seed and stronger single-seated-person prompt/negative prompt. The s01 + s02fix merge removed the duplicate person, but later review found segment 1 and segment 2 did not keep a consistent chair state, so this run is not accepted.

Two 30s segments reused the same fixed photovoltaic background prompt and the seated pure-color IP image. Quick review showed stable background and no duplicate body, but detailed review found hand-motion drift around final 49.5s-50.5s. The corresponding reference window is segment 2 19.5s-20.5s, where large sticker/location text covers the lower body and hand area. Conclusion: overlay text can contaminate KJ action/pose conditioning even when the final output does not redraw the text; clean the reference video before rerunning.

The run reused the previously accepted first 30s segment and reran only segment 2 after cleaning the 49.3s-52.0s hand-anchor overlay window in the 60s reference. The prior hand/multi-hand drift around final 49.5s-50.5s improved, but detailed review found a short-lived red point around final 29.6s near the right leg/chair area. Corresponding reference frames around 26s-30s contain red UI tokens such as checkmarks and a location bubble. Conclusion: overlay contamination is probabilistic and can manifest as extra hands, hand drift, red dots, or sticker residue; acceptances require both preflight reference-overlay review and postflight generated-artifact review.

implementation_variants.description: Split video into 10s segments, generate independently, then stitch with ffmpeg.
Adds tail-frame / continue-motion support on top of segmented v1.

Single-instance segmented implementation. Splits source video into 10s segments, carries 5 tail frames forward with continue_motion, downloads all segment outputs, and ffmpeg-concats the merged result.

Failed visual experiment. Anchors every 10s segment on the original reference image, cuts later segments with an overlap head, and merges downloaded outputs locally with ffmpeg xfade/acrossfade. It avoided v3 continue_motion identity carryover but broke cross-segment continuity because each segment re-generated a different composition.

validation_runs.notes: Initial remote submission executed only temp preview node 299 because v4 removed the required continue_motion_max_frames input. The live instance was rescued by resubmitting patched prompts that kept continue_motion_max_frames=5, removed non-save VHS preview outputs, and executed save_output node 341. The script was then patched and PrepareOnly verified. Later frame review showed the back half is unusable: s02 and s03 already re-generate the person, pose, chair, and framing differently before merge, so xfade/acrossfade cannot repair continuity.
